items.prompt: What does data lineage track at platform scale?
choices: Where data came from, how it was transformed, and which systems consume it
Only the font used in slide decks
items.explain: Section 12.4.1 defines lineage as origin, transforms, and dependents.

items.prompt: Example 12.20 records input table version, feature job ID, and model artifact URI when a training job starts.
items.explain: Example 12.20 describes automated capture of those identifiers for later audits.

items.prompt: What debugging use does Example 12.21 illustrate?
choices: Using a lineage UI to find which transform introduced an empty partition
Replacing lineage with random sampling only
items.explain: Example 12.21 shows walking filter/join chains to locate an empty partition.

items.prompt: What creates cross-platform lineage gaps in Example 12.22?
items.rubric: Mention missing connectors / lineage stopping at cloud, Spark, or ML-platform boundaries.
items.sample_answer: Missing connectors so lineage stops at object storage, Spark, or ML-platform boundaries.

items.prompt: Chapter 12 lineage tooling emphasizes enterprise catalogs such as Apache Atlas and Amundsen rather than only per-project DVC files.
items.explain: Section 12.4 contrasts enterprise metadata platforms with per-project DVC and paper-facing provenance.
